table_semantics.justification: Full table structure: <caption>, <thead>, <tbody>, <tfoot>. Plan names use <th scope='col'> and feature names use <th scope='row'>. Table is used correctly for two-dimensional tabular data with responsive overflow wrapper.

element_choice.justification:
CTAs are elements linking to appropriate URLs, plan cards are
  • items in
      , prices use (not headings). Minor issue: the brand name 'Forge' in the
      is wrapped in a

      rather than a more appropriate element like a or . The

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single <h1> ('Simple, transparent pricing'), <h2> for sections ('Available plans' visually hidden, 'Compare features' visible), <h3> for individual plan names — no skipped levels. Prices and the 'Most Popular' badge are not headings.

aria_discipline.justification: Mostly disciplined: aria-hidden on the visual badge prevents duplicate announcement, aria-labelledby on sections, descriptive aria-label on CTAs disambiguates repeated text. Minor misuse: aria-label on the <li> element for the Professional card has no semantic effect since listitems don't expose an accessible name; the 'Most Popular' info is adequately conveyed elsewhere (visually-hidden span in table header, CTA aria-label).

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link present as first focusable element, <header>, <main id='main-content' tabindex='-1'>, <footer> all used correctly. Two <section> elements each have aria-labelledby. No unnecessary landmarks added.

list_semantics.justification: Plan cards are correctly placed in a <ul> with <li> items, allowing screen readers to announce the count of plans.

content_realism.justification: Realistic brand name ('Forge'), plausible pricing ($9, $29, Custom), real feature names and values, copyright year set to 2026.

expectations_met: Feature comparison uses <table> for two-dimensional data
Plan names are <th scope='col'> in the header row
Feature names are <th scope='row'> in each row
'Most Popular' badge is accessible via visually-hidden text in the table header and CTA aria-labels
CTA elements are <a> links pointing to appropriate URLs
Plan names above the table use <h3> (correct level under <h2>)
Prices are not headings — marked up with inside
Boolean feature values (✓/✕) have aria-label text alternatives ('Included'/'Not included')
expectations_missed: Yes/No values are conveyed by Unicode symbols (✓/✕) with aria-label overrides rather than visible text ('Yes'/'No'); the expectation calls for visible text to be present alongside or instead of icons
additional_observations: Excellent use of overflow-x: auto wrapper for responsive table handling
The <style> block defining .visually-hidden is inside <body> rather than <head> — functionally works but is non-standard placement
aria-label on <li> for the Professional card is a no-op semantically but causes no harm
